# Effortless AWS

> Code-first AWS Lambda framework for TypeScript. Export handlers, deploy with one command.

Effortless AWS is a TypeScript framework where you define AWS resources (Lambda, DynamoDB, SQS, S3, SES, CloudFront) by exporting handler objects from `.ts` files. The framework handles all infrastructure — IAM roles, Lambda Function URLs, event source mappings, Lambda layers — automatically. No YAML, no CloudFormation, no state files.

- Package: `effortless-aws` (npm)
- CLI: `eff deploy`
- Docs: https://effortless-aws.website

## Project setup

```typescript
// effortless.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "effortless-aws";

export default defineConfig({
  name: "my-service",       // prefix for all AWS resources
  region: "eu-central-1",
  handlers: ["src/**/*.ts"], // glob patterns to discover handlers
  stage: "dev",              // default stage, override with --stage
  defaults: {
    memory: 256,             // MB (128–10240)
    timeout: "30 seconds",   // human-readable
    runtime: "nodejs24.x",
  },
});
```

All Lambdas run on ARM64 (Graviton2). Not configurable.

## Core concepts

### Handler = exported const

Every `export const x = define*({...})` creates AWS resources. The export name becomes the resource name.

### deps = automatic IAM wiring

Reference other handlers via `deps: { orders, uploads, mailer }`. The framework injects typed clients and creates IAM permissions automatically.

### config = SSM secrets

`config: { key: param("ssm/path") }` fetches secrets from SSM Parameter Store at cold start, caches them. SSM path: `/${project}/${stage}/${key}`.

### setup = cold-start factory

`setup: async ({ deps, config }) => ({ db: createPool(config.dbUrl) })` runs once per cold start. Return value is available as `ctx` in callbacks.

### schema = validation

Pass a validation function or `typed<T>()` for type-only. Validated data arrives as `data` arg in callbacks.

### static = bundled files

`static: ["src/templates/*.ejs"]` bundles files into Lambda ZIP. Access via `files.read("src/templates/invoice.ejs")`.

## Handler types

### defineApi — HTTP API

Creates Lambda + Function URL with built-in routing. Deploys a single Lambda that handles all routes internally — no API Gateway needed.

`defineApi` is the primary way to build HTTP APIs. It supports multiple GET routes and a POST command handler under a shared base path with shared deps/config/setup.

```typescript
import { defineApi, defineTable, typed, param } from "effortless-aws";
import { z } from "zod";

type User = { tag: string; name: string; email: string };

export const users = defineTable({ schema: typed<User>() });

// Discriminated union for POST commands
const Action = z.discriminatedUnion("action", [
  z.object({ action: z.literal("create"), name: z.string(), email: z.string() }),
  z.object({ action: z.literal("delete"), id: z.string() }),
]);

export default defineApi({
  basePath: "/api",                          // all routes prefixed with /api
  deps: { users },
  config: { dbUrl: param("database-url") },
  setup: async ({ deps, config }) => ({ pool: createPool(config.dbUrl) }),

  // GET routes — queries (path-based routing, no body)
  get: {
    "/users": async ({ req, deps }) => ({
      status: 200,
      body: await deps.users.queryByTag({ tag: "user" }),
    }),
    "/users/{id}": async ({ req, deps }) => ({
      status: 200,
      body: await deps.users.get({ pk: `USER#${req.params.id}`, sk: "PROFILE" }),
    }),
  },

  // POST — commands (single handler, discriminated union)
  schema: (input) => Action.parse(input),
  post: async ({ data, deps }) => {
    switch (data.action) {
      case "create": {
        const id = crypto.randomUUID();
        await deps.users.put({ pk: `USER#${id}`, sk: "PROFILE", data: { tag: "user", ...data } });
        return { status: 201, body: { id } };
      }
      case "delete": {
        await deps.users.delete({ pk: `USER#${data.id}`, sk: "PROFILE" });
        return { status: 200, body: { ok: true } };
      }
    }
  },
});
```

**Key features:**
- `basePath` — single Lambda handles all routes under this prefix
- `get` — record of `{ path: handler }` for query routes with `{param}` extraction
- `schema` + `post` — single POST handler with discriminated union for commands
- Both `get` and `post` are optional (you can use just one)
- Shared `deps`, `config`, `setup`, `static`, `onError` across all routes
- Unmatched routes get a 404 automatically
- Lambda Function URL with built-in CORS — no API Gateway overhead

**`req` object (available in all route handlers):**
- `req.method` — HTTP method
- `req.path` — request path
- `req.headers` — `Record<string, string | undefined>`
- `req.query` — query string parameters
- `req.params` — path parameters from `{param}` placeholders
- `req.body` — parsed body (JSON auto-parsed)
- `req.rawBody` — raw string body

### defineTable — DynamoDB table

Creates a DynamoDB table. Optionally creates a stream Lambda if `onRecord`/`onBatch` is provided.

**Single-table design with fixed envelope:**

| Attribute | Type   | Purpose                           |
|-----------|--------|-----------------------------------|
| `pk`      | String | Partition key                     |
| `sk`      | String | Sort key                          |
| `tag`     | String | Entity type (auto-extracted from data) |
| `data`    | Map    | Your domain data (`T`)            |
| `ttl`     | Number | Optional TTL timestamp            |

```typescript
import { defineTable, typed } from "effortless-aws";

type Order = { tag: string; amount: number; status: string };

// Resource-only (no Lambda, just a table)
export const orders = defineTable({
  schema: typed<Order>(),
});

// With stream handler
export const orders = defineTable({
  schema: typed<Order>(),
  tagField: "tag",           // default; change to "type" or "kind" if needed

  // Per-record processing (partial batch failures built in)
  onRecord: async ({ record, deps, config, ctx }) => {
    // record.eventName: "INSERT" | "MODIFY" | "REMOVE"
    // record.new?.data — new item data (typed as Order)
    // record.old?.data — previous item data
    // record.keys — { pk, sk }
  },

  // OR batch processing
  onBatch: async ({ records }) => { /* all records at once */ },

  // OR record + summary
  onRecord: async ({ record }) => { return { amount: record.new!.data.amount }; },
  onBatchComplete: async ({ results, failures }) => { /* aggregate */ },

  batchSize: 100,            // 1-10000, default varies
  deps: { notifications },
  config: { webhookUrl: param("webhook/url") },
  setup: async ({ deps, config }) => ({ /* cold-start init */ }),
  memory: 256,
  timeout: 30,
  logLevel: "info",
});
```

**TableClient API** (available via `deps`):

```typescript
// Put
await table.put({ pk: "USER#alice", sk: "PROFILE", data: { tag: "user", name: "Alice" } });
await table.put({ pk: "...", sk: "...", data: {...}, ttl: unixTimestamp }, { ifNotExists: true });

// Get
const item = await table.get({ pk: "USER#alice", sk: "PROFILE" });
// item.data.name → typed

// Delete
await table.delete({ pk: "USER#alice", sk: "PROFILE" });

// Query
const items = await table.query({ pk: "USER#alice", sk: { begins_with: "ORDER#" } });
const items = await table.query({
  pk: "USER#alice",
  sk: { begins_with: "ORDER#" },
  limit: 10,
  scanIndexForward: false,   // newest first
});

// Query by tag (GSI)
const users = await table.queryByTag({ tag: "user", limit: 50 });

// Update (auto-prefixes data. in DynamoDB expression)
await table.update({ pk: "USER#alice", sk: "ORDER#001" }, {
  set: { status: "shipped" },
  append: { events: ["shipped"] },
  remove: ["tempNotes"],
  tag: "shipped-order",
  ttl: unixTimestamp,        // or null to remove
});
```

### defineFifoQueue — SQS FIFO queue

Creates SQS FIFO queue + Lambda consumer.

```typescript
import { defineFifoQueue, typed } from "effortless-aws";

type OrderEvent = { orderId: string; action: "created" | "shipped" };

export const orderQueue = defineFifoQueue({
  schema: typed<OrderEvent>(),

  // Per-message (partial batch failures built in)
  onMessage: async ({ message, deps, config, ctx }) => {
    // message.body — typed as OrderEvent
    // message.messageId, message.messageGroupId, message.rawBody
  },

  // OR batch processing
  onBatch: async ({ messages }) => { /* all messages at once */ },

  batchSize: 10,              // 1-10, default: 10
  batchWindow: 10,            // seconds to wait, 0-300
  visibilityTimeout: 120,     // seconds before retry
  retentionPeriod: 86400,     // message retention in seconds
  contentBasedDeduplication: true,
  deps: { orders },
  config: { apiKey: param("api-key") },
  memory: 256,
  timeout: 30,
  logLevel: "info",
});
```

**Sending messages** (from another handler via `deps`):

```typescript
export const orders = defineApi({
  basePath: "/orders",
  deps: { orderQueue },
  post: async ({ deps }) => {
    await deps.orderQueue.send({
      body: { orderId: "123", action: "created" },
      messageGroupId: "order-123",
    });
    return { status: 200, body: { ok: true } };
  },
});
```

### defineBucket — S3 bucket

Creates an S3 bucket. Optionally creates event Lambda.

```typescript
import { defineBucket } from "effortless-aws";

// Resource-only
export const uploads = defineBucket({});

// With event handlers
export const images = defineBucket({
  prefix: "uploads/",       // filter by key prefix
  suffix: ".jpg",            // filter by key suffix

  onObjectCreated: async ({ event, bucket, deps }) => {
    // event.key, event.size, event.eventName, event.eTag, event.eventTime
    // bucket — BucketClient for this bucket
    const file = await bucket.get(event.key);
  },

  onObjectRemoved: async ({ event }) => {
    await removeFromIndex(event.key);
  },

  deps: { records },
  config: { apiKey: param("api-key") },
  memory: 256,
  timeout: 30,
});
```

**BucketClient API** (available via `deps`):

```typescript
await bucket.put("path/file.csv", content);
await bucket.put("path/photo.jpg", buffer, { contentType: "image/jpeg" });
const file = await bucket.get("path/file.csv");  // { body: Buffer, contentType?: string } | undefined
await bucket.delete("path/file.csv");
const files = await bucket.list("prefix/");       // [{ key, size, lastModified }]
```

### defineMailer — SES email

Creates SES email identity with DKIM. Resource-only, used via `deps`.

```typescript
import { defineMailer } from "effortless-aws";

export const mailer = defineMailer({ domain: "myapp.com" });
```

**EmailClient API** (available via `deps`):

```typescript
await deps.mailer.send({
  from: "hello@myapp.com",
  to: "user@example.com",           // or ["a@b.com", "c@d.com"]
  subject: "Welcome!",
  html: "<h1>Welcome!</h1>",        // at least one of html/text required
  text: "Welcome!",
});
```

### defineApp — SSR framework deployment

Creates CloudFront + Lambda Function URL + S3 for SSR frameworks (Nuxt, Next.js via OpenNext).

```typescript
import { defineApp } from "effortless-aws";

export const frontend = defineApp({
  server: ".output/server",   // directory with index.mjs Lambda handler
  assets: ".output/public",   // static assets → S3
  build: "nuxt build",        // build command (runs before deploy)
  domain: "app.example.com",  // optional custom domain (needs ACM cert in us-east-1)
  memory: 1024,
});

// Stage-specific domains
export const app = defineApp({
  server: ".output/server",
  assets: ".output/public",
  build: "nuxt build",
  domain: {
    prod: "app.example.com",
    staging: "staging.example.com",
  },
});
```

**Supported frameworks:**
- Nuxt (native, set `NITRO_PRESET=aws-lambda`)
- Next.js (via OpenNext: `server: ".open-next/server-function"`, `assets: ".open-next/assets"`, `build: "npx open-next build"`)
- Any Nitro-based framework (Analog, Vinxi)
- Custom handler (export `handler(event, context)` from `index.mjs`)

### defineStaticSite — static site / SPA on CloudFront

Creates S3 + CloudFront distribution.

```typescript
import { defineStaticSite } from "effortless-aws";

export const docs = defineStaticSite({
  dir: "dist",                // directory with built static files
  build: "npm run build",     // build command
  domain: "docs.example.com", // optional custom domain
  spa: true,                  // SPA mode: all routes → index.html
  errorPage: "404.html",      // custom error page (non-SPA only)

  // API proxying (eliminates CORS)
  routes: {
    "/api/*": apiHandler,     // proxy to API Gateway
  },

  // Edge authentication (Lambda@Edge)
  middleware: async (request) => {
    // request: { uri, method, querystring, headers, cookies }
    if (!request.cookies.session) {
      return { redirect: "https://example.com/login" };
    }
    // return nothing → serve the page
    // return { status: 403, body: "Denied" } → block
  },
});
```

## CLI commands

```bash
eff deploy                         # deploy all handlers
eff deploy ./src/api.ts            # deploy specific file
eff deploy createUser              # deploy specific handler
eff deploy --stage prod            # deploy to production

eff status                         # compare code vs deployed resources
eff logs processOrder --tail       # stream CloudWatch logs
eff logs processOrder --since 1h   # logs from last hour

eff config                         # interactive SSM parameter setup
eff config list                    # show all params and status
eff config set stripe/secret-key   # set specific parameter

eff cleanup                        # preview orphaned resources
eff cleanup --handler oldHandler --all  # delete specific handler
eff cleanup --all                  # delete all resources
eff cleanup --layer --all          # clean up layer versions
eff cleanup --roles --all          # clean up orphaned IAM roles

eff layer                          # show dependency layer info
eff layer --build                  # build layer locally
```

All commands accept `--project`, `--stage`, `--region`, `--verbose`.

## Key patterns and conventions

### Naming

All AWS resources follow `${project}-${stage}-${handlerName}`:
- Lambda: `my-service-dev-createUser`
- DynamoDB: `my-service-dev-orders`
- SQS FIFO: `my-service-dev-orderQueue.fifo`
- Function URL: auto-created per Lambda (no separate name)

### Stage isolation

Each stage gets fully independent resources. No shared infrastructure between `dev` and `prod`.

### No state files

Resources are discovered via AWS resource tags (not CloudFormation/Terraform state). Tags: `effortless-project`, `effortless-stage`, `effortless-handler`, `effortless-component`.

### Lambda layer

Production `dependencies` from `package.json` are bundled into a shared Lambda Layer. AWS SDK v3 packages are excluded (provided by Lambda runtime). Layer is hash-versioned — only re-uploaded when deps change.

### Type inference

All generics are inferred automatically. Never specify generics manually:

```typescript
export const orders = defineTable({
  schema: typed<Order>(),                       // T = Order
  deps: { users },                              // D inferred
  config: { threshold: param("threshold", Number) }, // P inferred, Number transform applied
  setup: async ({ config }) => ({               // C = return type
    db: createPool(config.threshold),
  }),
  onRecord: async ({ record, ctx, deps, config }) => {
    // record.new?.data is Order
    // ctx is { db: Pool }
    // deps.users is TableClient<User>
    // config.threshold is number
  },
});
```

### Callback args

Handler callbacks receive a single object with relevant args. Args are conditionally present based on configuration:
- `req` — HTTP request (defineApi)
- `data` — validated body (when schema is set)
- `deps` — typed dep clients (when deps is set)
- `config` — resolved SSM values (when config is set)
- `ctx` — setup return value (when setup is set)
- `files` — static file reader (when static is set)
- `record` / `records` / `message` / `messages` — event data (type-specific)
- `event` / `bucket` — S3 event data (defineBucket)

### param() with transforms

```typescript
config: {
  apiKey: param("api-key"),                    // string
  port: param("port", Number),                 // number (transform applied)
  config: param("app-config", JSON.parse),     // parsed JSON
}
```

### typed() helper

Type-only schema (no runtime validation):
```typescript
schema: typed<MyType>()
```

Runtime validation with any library (e.g. Zod):
```typescript
schema: (input) => MySchema.parse(input)
```
